rules.description: CRITICAL IMPLEMENTATION GAP: No code exists to close the A/B feedback loop. Without this, 38 active gates produce zero learning signal regardless of how long bot runs.
rules.problem: All treatment_wr values in graduated_rules.json are null. Rules cannot graduate or be rejected by evidence because trade outcomes are never matched back to their A/B cohort assignment.

rules.expected_impact: Enables ALL 38 A/B rules to graduate or be rejected. Converts learning system from theoretical to empirical.
rules.proposed_fix: evaluate_active_fixes() implemented in auto_fix_pipeline - full A/B split logic with auto-revert (-3pp) and promote (+2pp) thresholds

rules.proposed_fix: Tag each live trade with active A/B cohort membership. After close, read trade outcome and update treatment_wr for matching rule in graduated_rules.json. Implement in feedback/loop.py post-trade hook.
rules.implementation_target: bot/feedback/loop.py — post-trade hook: tag trade with active A/B rule cohort membership; on close, update treatment_wr in graduated_rules.json

rules.description: Escalate SOL_SHORT_edge_promote from 20% gate to 50%: confirmed 63.7% WR, +$5807 PnL on 179 trades.
rules.problem: SOL_SHORT is the #1 profit driver in the 965-trade dataset. SOL_SHORT_edge_promote A/B rule at 20% gate since 2026-04-25 — confidence 90%, sample 179 >= min_sample 30. Meets graduation criteria. Escalate gate.
rules.proposed_fix: Update SOL_SHORT_edge_promote gate_percentage from 20 to 50. Maintain +5pt confidence and 1.1x size boost in treatment cohort.
rules.block_reason: Live paper trading data (52 actual trades) shows SOL WR=34.6%, -$176.24 — the single largest loss driver. Escalating SOL_SHORT gate would amplify a losing setup. Blocked by 2026-04-25 hourly run. Re-evaluate after 30+ live trades in clearly trending regime show SOL WR > 55%.

rules.description: CRITICAL: SOL live paper WR=34.6% on 52 trades (-$176.24) contradicts backtest claim of 63.7% WR. SOL is the PRIMARY LOSS DRIVER.
rules.problem: Rules SOL_SHORT_edge_promote and SOL_SHORT_priority_boost were promoting SOL SHORT based on 965-trade backtest. Live paper trading shows SOL is the worst performing asset (64% of all PnL losses, 82.7% SL rate). Backtest performance did not transfer to live execution.
rules.fix_applied: APPLIED: SOL_SHORT_edge_promote and SOL_SHORT_priority_boost status changed to SUSPENDED_LIVE_CONTRADICTION. SOL_SHORT_GATE_ESCALATE blocked. SOL de-prioritized.
rules.reconciliation_hypothesis: Live paper trading uses real spreads, slippage, and current market conditions. Backtest may have used favorable historical data with lower SOL volatility or better fill conditions. SOL's 82.7% SL rate indicates entries are systematically hitting noise before trend develops.
rules.reactivation_criteria: Re-activate SOL_SHORT_edge_promote when: (1) 30+ consecutive live SOL trades show WR > 55%; (2) bot running in trending-only regime filter; (3) stop widths verified >= 1.0% for SOL signals.

rules.description: CRITICAL AUDIT: Backtest shows conf 60-70% = 53.4% WR (BEST), conf 90%+ = 42.1% WR (WORST). Live paper claims opposite: 90%+ is primary profit driver. Confidence calibration signal is unresolvable without data audit.
rules.problem: Backtest (317 trades): 60-70%=53.4%, 70-80%=48.9%, 80-90%=47.0%, 90%+=42.1%. Live paper rules built on: 60-70%=46.3% WR WORST, 90%+=56% WR BEST. This inversion means ALL confidence-routing rules (CONF_70_HARD_FLOOR, confidence_60_70_ROUTING_FIX, etc.) may be targeting the wrong direction. Rules could be HURTING not helping.
rules.fix_applied: AUDIT_FREEZE: do not escalate any confidence-routing rule gates beyond current levels until data sources reconciled. This rule blocks escalation of CONF_70_HARD_FLOOR, CONF_60_70_all_block, and confidence_60_70_ROUTING_FIX.
rules.action: FREEZE: Do not escalate confidence-routing rules. Investigate whether backtest_10d.csv and live paper trades use same confidence calibration model. Backtest llm_confidence field is empty — may mean confidence in backtest was assigned differently.
rules.resolution: Full 965-trade analysis confirms 60-70% WR=46.3% (WORST), 90%+ WR=56% (BEST by EV). No inversion. Live and backtest agree. Freeze lifted. See CONF_CALIBRATION_RESOLVED.
rules.investigation_note: backtest_10d.csv llm_confidence column is empty for all rows — backtest uses 'confidence' column which is signal confidence, not LLM-assigned confidence. Live paper may be using LLM confidence. These are DIFFERENT metrics being compared as if same.
rules.resolution_path: Separate signal confidence (from strategies) vs LLM confidence (from agent pipeline). Re-run bin analysis only on live trades with LLM confidence populated.

Primary drivers: (1) 74% LONG bias with LONG WR=30% (SIDE_BIAS_ENFORCEMENT_V2 applied), (2) likely non-trending regime contamination (illiquid WR=28.1%, ranging=25%). The two root causes compound: most LONG trades are taken in illiquid/ranging regimes where market structure is unfavorable.

TRENDING_MIN_HOLD_2H_GATE_UPGRADE (gate=50%) addresses regime contamination. No additional direct rule added — 29 ensemble rules already exist; new rule risks conflicts.

rules.source: perpetual_deep_dive_2026_05_19_0307 — forensics vs meta_learning contradiction (conf=0.80 bias insights vs +$0.79/trade forensics at 3-5x)
rules.description: AUDIT: meta_learning bias insights (conf=0.80) claim >2.1x leverage has avg PnL=-$3.46 vs -$0.40 for smaller. This CONTRADICTS execution forensics which shows 3-5x leverage = +$0.79/trade on 68 trades. Root cause: meta_learning bins ALL positions >2.1x together, including the 6-10x tail that forensics shows is -$0.38/trade on 91 trades.
rules.problem: Meta_learning size bias insights use single threshold cutoffs (>1.5x, >2.0x, >2.1x, >5.0x) that mix profitable mid-leverage (3-5x: +$0.79/trade) with losing high-leverage (6-10x: -$0.38/trade). This creates false negative signal for the 3-5x range which is actually the BEST leverage band per forensics.
rules.fix_applied: DOCUMENTATION + DEV TASK: (1) No new trading rule applied — HIGH_LEV_10X_FORENSICS_BLOCK already correctly blocks >6x. (2) Dev task added: fix meta_learning insight generator to use binned leverage tiers (1-2x, 3-5x, 6-10x, >10x) instead of single 'larger vs smaller' threshold. This prevents future false bias signals about the profitable 3-5x range. rules_added_2026_05_03_2012.description: CRITICAL IMPLEMENTATION GAP: No code exists to close the A/B feedback loop. Without this, 38 active gates produce zero learning signal regardless of how long bot runs.
rules_added_2026_05_03_2012.problem: All treatment_wr values in graduated_rules.json are null. Rules cannot graduate or be rejected by evidence because trade outcomes are never matched back to their A/B cohort assignment.
rules_added_2026_05_03_2012.proposed_implementation: In bot/feedback/loop.py post-trade hook: (1) On trade OPEN: check each A/B_ACTIVE rule, assign cohort (treatment vs control) via hash(trade_id, rule_id) % 100 < gate_percentage, tag trade with active cohorts. (2) On trade CLOSE: read trade tags, update treatment_wr per rule via running average. (3) If treatment_wr - baseline_wr > 0.05 and n >= min_sample_to_graduate: auto-graduate rule to full gate.
rules_added_2026_05_03_2012.expected_impact: Enables ALL 38 A/B rules to graduate or be rejected. Converts learning system from theoretical to empirical.
